title: spicy roasted venezuelan cayenne pepper stew
description: a rich and spicy stew from venezuela featuring roasted cayenne peppers, tender beef, and a silky texture. serve over rice to soak up the flavorful broth.
instructions: season the short ribs with salt, black pepper, and a pinch of cayenne pepper.

in a hot pot, heat a couple of tablespoons of vegetable oil over medium-high heat. sear the short ribs until browned on all sides, about 3-4 minutes per side. transfer the short ribs to a plate and set aside.
in the same pot, add another tablespoon of oil and sauté the chopped onion until translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
add the chopped cayenne peppers and sauté for another minute, until fragrant.
add the beef broth, tomato paste, cumin, and smoked paprika to the pot. stir to combine and bring to a boil.
return the short ribs to the pot, cover, and transfer to the preheated oven.
bake for 2-3 hours, or until the meat is tender and falls off the bone.
remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
using two forks, shred the short ribs and return them to the pot. stir to combine and season with salt and black pepper to taste.
serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro and a dollop of sour cream, if desired.
